# 'downstack merge --no-wait' skips merge-propagation polling
# only for a single branch.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2024-04-05T16:40:32Z'

# setup
cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'

# set up a fake GitHub remote
shamhub init
shamhub new origin alice/example.git
shamhub register alice
git push origin main

env SHAMHUB_USERNAME=alice
gs auth login

# create a stack: main -> feature1 -> feature2
git add feature1.txt
gs bc feature1 -m 'Add feature 1'
git add feature2.txt
gs bc feature2 -m 'Add feature 2'

# submit the stack
gs downstack submit --fill
stderr 'Created #1'
stderr 'Created #2'

# --no-wait is rejected for multiple branches
# because it cannot run the local merge queue without waiting.
env ROBOT_INPUT=$WORK/robot-merge.golden ROBOT_OUTPUT=$WORK/robot-merge.actual
! gs downstack merge --no-wait
stderr '--no-wait can merge only one branch'

# merge a single branch with --no-wait:
# the merge request is sent,
# but the command does not poll for merge propagation.
gs bco feature1
gs downstack merge --no-wait
stderr 'Merging feature1'
stderr 'All 1 change.s. merged.'
git branch --list feature1
stdout 'feature1'
git branch --list feature2
stdout 'feature2'

-- repo/feature1.txt --
This is feature 1
-- repo/feature2.txt --
This is feature 2
-- robot-merge.golden --
===
> Merge 1 change(s) bottom-up?: [Y/n]
>   feature1 (#1)
true
